---
title: "Wetland_vars_selection_Local"
author: "Michael Krochta"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

*loading in data, defining predictor groups, cor threshold*
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(broom)
library(purrr)
library(caret)
library(tidyr)

# Read data 
df <- read.csv("C:/Users/mbkro/OneDrive/Documents/wetland-ml-models/data/daily_wetlands_dataset.csv")

# Responses
responses <- c("mean_WT", "max_WT", "range_WT")

# Predictor groups (same as global)
predictor_groups <- list(
  Solar    = c("Solar_daily", "Solar_2d", "Solar_3d", "Solar_7d"),
  WL_mean  = c("WL_daily_mean", "WL_mean_2day", "WL_mean_3day", "WL_mean_7day"),
  WL_max   = c("WL_daily_max", "WL_max_2day", "WL_max_3day", "WL_max_7day"),
  WL_min   = c("WL_daily_min", "WL_min_2day", "WL_min_3day", "WL_min_7day"),
  Air_tmean= c("Air_tmean", "Air_tmean_2day", "Air_tmean_3day", "Air_tmean_7day"),
  Air_tmax = c("Air_tmax", "Air_tmax_2day", "Air_tmax_3day", "Air_tmax_7day"),
  Air_tmin = c("Air_tmin", "Air_tmin_2day", "Air_tmin_3day", "Air_tmin_7day"),
  GWT_mean = c("GWT_mean", "GWT_mean_2day", "GWT_mean_3day", "GWT_mean_7day"),
  GWT_max  = c("GWT_max", "GWT_max_2day", "GWT_max_3day", "GWT_max_7day"),
  GWT_min  = c("GWT_min", "GWT_min_2day", "GWT_min_3day", "GWT_min_7day"),
  GWL_mean = c("GWL_mean", "GWL_mean_2day", "GWL_mean_3day", "GWL_mean_7day"),
  GWL_max  = c("GWL_max", "GWL_max_2day", "GWL_max_3day", "GWL_max_7day"),
  GWL_min  = c("GWL_min", "GWL_min_2day", "GWL_min_3day", "GWL_min_7day"),
  ppt      = c("ppt", "ppt_7day")
)

# Static predictors
static_predictors <- c(
  "Air_trange", "WL_daily_range", "GWT_range", "GWL_range", "Water_area_m2_3d",
  "Elev_m", "Size_km2"
)

# Correlation threshold 
cor_threshold <- 0.90
```

*Fitting site-level lms to find best preds, removing collinear or near-zero-vars preds*
```{r}
# For a single site-data frame and one response, pick best predictor per group
find_best_predictors_site <- function(df_site, response_var) {
  map_df(names(predictor_groups), function(gname) {
    vars <- intersect(predictor_groups[[gname]], names(df_site))
    if (length(vars) == 0) return(tibble(group = character(), predictor = character(), adj.r.squared = double()))
    
    # Fit univariate LMs safely, skip if not enough data
    metrics <- vars %>%
      set_names() %>%
      map(~ {
        # Require at least two non-NA rows for response and this predictor
        dat <- df_site %>% select(any_of(c(response_var, .x))) %>% drop_na()
        if (nrow(dat) < 3) return(NULL)  # too few rows to be meaningful
        fit <- lm(reformulate(.x, response_var), data = dat, na.action = na.exclude)
        glance(fit) %>% mutate(predictor = .x)
      }) %>%
      compact() %>%
      list_rbind()
    
    if (nrow(metrics) == 0) {
      tibble(group = character(), predictor = character(), adj.r.squared = double())
    } else {
      best <- metrics %>% slice_max(adj.r.squared, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
        transmute(group = gname, predictor, adj.r.squared)
      best
    }
  })
}

remove_collinear_site <- function(df_site_model, response_var, threshold = cor_threshold) {
  exclude_cols <- c(response_var, "Site", "Datetime")

  # Numeric predictors only
  pred_names <- setdiff(names(df_site_model), exclude_cols)
  num_pred_names <- df_site_model %>%
    dplyr::select(dplyr::any_of(pred_names)) %>%
    dplyr::select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
    names()

  # Drop ultra-sparse predictors (<3 non-NA obs cannot form reliable correlations)
  if (length(num_pred_names) == 0) {
    return(list(clean_df = df_site_model, removed = character(0)))
  }
  nn <- vapply(df_site_model[num_pred_names], function(x) sum(!is.na(x)), integer(1))
  sparse_drop <- names(nn[nn < 3])
  keep <- setdiff(num_pred_names, sparse_drop)

  if (length(keep) < 2) {
    clean_df <- df_site_model %>% dplyr::select(-dplyr::any_of(sparse_drop))
    return(list(clean_df = clean_df, removed = sparse_drop))
  }

  # Drop near-zero-variance predictors within site
  nzv_idx  <- caret::nearZeroVar(df_site_model[keep], saveMetrics = FALSE)
  nzv_drop <- if (length(nzv_idx)) keep[nzv_idx] else character(0)
  keep2    <- setdiff(keep, nzv_drop)

  if (length(keep2) < 2) {
    clean_df <- df_site_model %>% dplyr::select(-dplyr::any_of(c(sparse_drop, nzv_drop)))
    return(list(clean_df = clean_df, removed = c(sparse_drop, nzv_drop)))
  }

  # Correlation matrix with robust handling of NA/Inf
  cor_matrix <- stats::cor(df_site_model[keep2], use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
  diag(cor_matrix) <- 0
  cor_matrix[!is.finite(cor_matrix)] <- 0  # allow pruning on finite pairs

  to_drop <- caret::findCorrelation(cor_matrix, cutoff = threshold, names = TRUE, exact = TRUE)

  removed  <- union(c(sparse_drop, nzv_drop), to_drop)
  clean_df <- df_site_model %>% dplyr::select(-dplyr::any_of(removed))
  list(clean_df = clean_df, removed = removed)
}

```

*Building output datasets for each site and model*
```{r}
# Ensure Datetime exists; if it's missing, create a placeholder to keep interface consistent
if (!"Datetime" %in% names(df)) df$Datetime <- NA

sites <- sort(unique(df$Site))

# Container outputs
site_response_data <- list()   # final model-ready data frames per Site × response
site_selection_log <- tibble() # tidy log of kept/removed per Site × response

for (s in sites) {
  df_site <- df %>% filter(Site == s)
  site_response_data[[s]] <- list()
  
  for (resp in responses) {
    # Skip if response not present
    if (!resp %in% names(df_site)) next
    
    # Step 1: best predictor per group for this Site × response
    best_tbl <- find_best_predictors_site(df_site, resp)
    
    # Defensive: coerce predictor names to character and filter NA/empty
    if (nrow(best_tbl) == 0) {
      chosen <- character(0)
    } else {
      chosen <- best_tbl %>%
        mutate(predictor = as.character(predictor)) %>%
        filter(!is.na(predictor), nzchar(predictor)) %>%
        pull(predictor) %>%
        unique()
    }
    
    # Combine with static predictors, only those present
    chosen_all <- c(chosen, static_predictors) %>%
      unique() %>%
      intersect(names(df_site))
    
    # Build site/response data frame (keep Site, Datetime, response)
    base_cols <- c("Site", "Datetime", resp)
    df_site_model <- df_site %>% select(any_of(c(base_cols, chosen_all)))
    
    # Step 2: remove collinear predictors within this site
    col_res  <- remove_collinear_site(df_site_model, response_var = resp, threshold = cor_threshold)
    final_df <- col_res$clean_df
    removed  <- col_res$removed
    
    # Record outputs
    site_response_data[[s]][[resp]] <- final_df
    
    # Compute kept from the final_df to reflect post-pruning set
    kept <- setdiff(names(final_df), base_cols)
    
    site_selection_log <- bind_rows(
      site_selection_log,
      tibble(
        Site               = s,
        response           = resp,
        kept_predictors    = list(kept),
        removed_predictors = list(removed)
      )
    )
  }
}
```

*double checking correlations*
```{r}
check_corr <- function(df, resp, thr = 0.90) {
  ex <- c("Site","Datetime", resp)
  X  <- df %>% dplyr::select(-dplyr::any_of(ex)) %>% dplyr::select(where(is.numeric))
  if (ncol(X) < 2) return(list(ok = TRUE, pairs = tibble()))
  R <- stats::cor(X, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
  diag(R) <- NA
  bad <- as.data.frame(as.table(R), stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
    dplyr::rename(var1 = Var1, var2 = Var2, r = Freq) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(r), abs(r) > thr, var1 < var2)
  list(ok = nrow(bad) == 0, pairs = tibble::as_tibble(bad))
}

# Scan all Site × response; list any problematic pairs
bad_list <- list()
for (s in names(site_response_data)) {
  for (resp in names(site_response_data[[s]])) {
    res <- check_corr(site_response_data[[s]][[resp]], resp, thr = 0.90)
    if (!res$ok) {
      bad_list[[length(bad_list)+1]] <- dplyr::bind_cols(
        tibble::tibble(Site = s, response = resp), res$pairs
      )
    }
  }
}
bad_pairs <- if (length(bad_list)) dplyr::bind_rows(bad_list) else tibble::tibble()
bad_pairs  # should be empty after the fix


```
*saving output files*
```{r}
# Write per-site CSVs  
output_dir <- "C:/Users/mbkro/OneDrive/Documents/wetland-ml-models/data/local_mod_data"

walk(names(site_response_data), function(s) {
   walk(names(site_response_data[[s]]), function(resp) {
     fn <- file.path(output_dir, paste0(s, "_", resp, "_model_data.csv"))
     write.csv(site_response_data[[s]][[resp]], fn, row.names = FALSE)
   })
 })
```

*Tables of selected predictors per Site × response*
```{r}
# Long table: one row per kept predictor (no list cols)
selected_long <- site_selection_log %>%
  mutate(response = dplyr::recode(response,
                                  mean_WT = "Mean",
                                  max_WT  = "Max",
                                  range_WT= "Range")) %>%
  # keep only what we need to unnest; drop other list cols
  select(Site, response, kept_predictors) %>%
  # drop rows where kept_predictors is NULL or length 0
  filter(lengths(kept_predictors) > 0) %>%
  # unnest character vectors into rows
  tidyr::unnest(kept_predictors) %>%
  rename(predictor = kept_predictors) %>%
  filter(!is.na(predictor), nzchar(predictor)) %>%
  arrange(Site, response, predictor)


# Save to CSVs 
out_dir <- "C:/Users/mbkro/OneDrive/Documents/wetland-ml-models/data/local_mod_data"

# Coerce to plain data.frame to be extra safe for write.csv
write.csv(as.data.frame(selected_long),   file.path(out_dir, "site_model_selected_predictors_long.csv"),   row.names = FALSE)

```
